title: buddleja corrugata
intro: Buddleja corrugata, also known as the corrugated butterfly bush, is a flowering plant species native to the western regions of South America. It is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The leaves and flowers of Buddleja corrugata can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, making it a versatile plant for herbal remedies.
